behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real vision-model judgment of explicitness; a stubbed classifier would hide misclassification failures. Rejected Workflow: behavior text describes one agentic decision (image classification) feeding one hold/publish gate, not a multi-agentic sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Submit real explicit images through the moderation endpoint using the production/staging vision model and assert each submission is marked held-for-review and not visible in published feed readback.

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: This is model robustness/quality against adversarial text variants; deterministic mocks always pass and miss true failure modes. Rejected Workflow: single moderation model decision per post, no multi-step agent pipeline in the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Run a corpus of obfuscated hate-speech samples against the real text moderation model and verify they are flagged/held at an acceptable recall threshold.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: Behavior requires proving an externally visible side effect (published, not dropped), which needs round-trip observation across the real boundary. Rejected Agentic: behavior focuses on publish-path orchestration after checks pass, not on model output quality; pass results can be deterministically forced for this test.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a known-safe post through the real submission API and verify via real readback (feed/query API) that it becomes published and is neither in hold queue nor missing.

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly measures a full multi-step pipeline with multiple agentic calls in sequence under end-to-end timing constraints; lower-level tests miss cumulative latency and sequencing effects.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end submissions that invoke real image and text models plus final decision path, and assert p95 end-to-end latency from submit to final state is <=5s under representative peak-hour load.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: This is an external error-path contract with real dependency unavailability and externally observable queue side effect; real network/API failure handling must be validated. Rejected Agentic: no dependence on model judgment quality, only outage handling behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Induce real vision-service unavailability (timeout/5xx) in a system environment, submit image posts, and verify they land in retry queue and are not auto-approved/published.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: Consistency here depends on repeated agentic decisions (or agentic decision plus short-window state reuse) across sequential submissions; single-call agentic tests can miss cross-step drift/flapping.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same borderline image repeatedly within the defined window using the real moderation stack and assert decision consistency across attempts (or documented bounded variance rule) with observed final states.
